sections.name: Setting up a cloud solution environment
sections.description: Projects, accounts, billing, resource hierarchy, APIs, quotas

sections.name: Planning and implementing a cloud solution
Ensuring successful operation of a cloud solution
sections.description: Managing compute, storage, networking, monitoring and logging

sections.name: Configuring access and security
sections.description: IAM, service accounts, Workload Identity Federation

questions.text: You need to create a resource hierarchy for a new Google Cloud organization. Which resource is the top-level node in the hierarchy?
questions.explanation: The Organization resource is the top-level node in the Google Cloud resource hierarchy, representing a company. Folders and projects exist beneath it.

questions.text: You want to restrict all resources in your organization to specific regions. What should you use?
questions.explanation: Organization policy constraints (e.g., constraints/gcp.resourceLocations) allow you to restrict which regions resources can be created in across the entire organization.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid methods for managing users and groups in Cloud Identity? (Select two)
options: Automated sync with Google Cloud Directory Sync (GCDS)
questions.explanation: Users can be managed manually via the Admin Console or automated using GCDS to sync from an existing directory like Active Directory or LDAP.

questions.text: You need to enable the Compute Engine API for a project. Which gcloud command do you use?
questions.explanation: The gcloud services enable command is used to enable APIs within a project. The full API name (e.g., compute.googleapis.com) must be specified.

questions.text: Your project has reached the quota limit for Compute Engine CPUs. What should you do?
options: Request a quota increase from the Google Cloud Console
Contact Google Cloud sales to purchase more CPUs
Wait for the quota to automatically increase
questions.explanation: You can request a quota increase directly from the IAM & Admin > Quotas page in the Google Cloud Console. Quotas do not automatically increase.

questions.text: You need to allow external identity providers to access Google Cloud resources without creating Google accounts. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Workforce Identity Federation allows external identity providers (like Azure AD or Okta) to authenticate users to Google Cloud without requiring Google accounts.

questions.text: You want to set up billing alerts when spending exceeds 80% of the monthly budget. What should you create?
options: A budget with alert threshold rules
questions.explanation: Billing budgets allow you to set threshold rules (e.g., 50%, 80%, 100%) that trigger alerts via email or Pub/Sub notifications when spending exceeds those percentages.

questions.text: How do you export billing data for analysis in BigQuery?
options: Enable billing export in the Billing Account settings and select a BigQuery dataset
Run a scheduled query in BigQuery to pull billing data
Use Cloud Scheduler to export billing CSV files
Configure a Dataflow pipeline from the billing API
questions.explanation: Billing export is configured in the Billing Account settings where you specify a BigQuery dataset. Google Cloud then automatically exports detailed billing data to that dataset.

questions.text: You need to verify whether a specific Google Cloud product is available in the asia-southeast1 region. Where do you check?
questions.explanation: The Google Cloud locations page (cloud.google.com/about/locations) shows product availability across all regions and zones.

questions.text: You want to use Gemini Cloud Assist to analyze your organization's deployed resources. What must you first configure?
questions.explanation: Cloud Asset Inventory must be configured to provide Gemini Cloud Assist with visibility into your deployed resources for analysis and recommendations.

questions.text: You need to run a containerized web application that scales to zero when there is no traffic. Which compute service should you use?
questions.explanation: Cloud Run is a serverless container platform that automatically scales to zero when there are no requests, and scales up based on incoming traffic.

questions.text: You are deploying a GKE cluster that should automatically manage node provisioning and scaling. Which mode should you use?
options: GKE Standard with manual node pools
questions.explanation: GKE Autopilot automatically manages the underlying infrastructure including node provisioning, scaling, and security configurations.

questions.text: Which storage option should you use for a Compute Engine instance that requires high-performance block storage shared across multiple VMs in the same zone?
questions.explanation: Google Cloud Hyperdisk provides high-performance block storage with configurable IOPS and throughput, and supports multi-writer mode for sharing across VMs.

questions.text: You want to create a managed instance group that automatically scales based on CPU utilization. What do you need to create first?
questions.explanation: An instance template defines the machine type, boot disk, and configuration used by a managed instance group to create identical VM instances for autoscaling.

questions.text: You need to deploy a function that triggers when a new object is uploaded to a Cloud Storage bucket. Which service should you use?
options: Cloud Run functions with Eventarc trigger
Compute Engine with a cron job
questions.explanation: Cloud Run functions (formerly Cloud Functions) can be triggered by Cloud Storage events via Eventarc, executing code automatically when objects are created or modified.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid considerations when choosing between GPUs and TPUs? (Select two)
options: TPUs are optimized for large-scale ML training workloads
GPUs are better suited for general-purpose parallel computing
TPUs are cheaper than GPUs for all workloads
GPUs cannot be attached to Compute Engine instances
questions.explanation: TPUs are custom ASICs designed for large-scale ML training (especially TensorFlow), while GPUs are more versatile for general parallel computing, rendering, and ML inference.

questions.text: You need a globally distributed, strongly consistent relational database for a financial application. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Cloud Spanner provides a globally distributed, horizontally scalable, strongly consistent relational database — ideal for financial and mission-critical applications.

questions.text: You want to store infrequently accessed data that may be needed once a quarter. Which Cloud Storage class is most cost-effective?
questions.explanation: Coldline storage is designed for data accessed at most once a quarter. It has lower storage costs than Standard/Nearline but higher retrieval costs.

questions.text: You need to transfer 50 TB of data from an on-premises data center to Cloud Storage. Which service is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Storage Transfer Service is designed for large-scale online data transfers to Cloud Storage from various sources including on-premises, other clouds, or HTTP/HTTPS locations.

questions.text: You need to create a VPC that allows you to define your own subnet IP ranges. Which VPC mode should you use?
questions.explanation: Custom mode VPC gives you full control over subnet creation and IP address ranges, unlike auto mode which automatically creates subnets in each region.

questions.text: You need to allow two VPC networks in different projects to communicate privately without using external IPs. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: VPC Network Peering allows private connectivity between VPC networks across different projects or organizations using internal IP addresses without a VPN or external IPs.

questions.text: You want to create a firewall rule that allows HTTP traffic from the internet to instances tagged 'web-server'. Which attributes do you configure? (Select three)
questions.explanation: An ingress rule with source 0.0.0.0/0 (all internet traffic) and target tag 'web-server' will allow HTTP traffic from the internet to tagged instances.

questions.text: You need to deploy infrastructure repeatably across multiple environments. Which tool should you use?
questions.explanation: Terraform is an Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool that allows you to define, provision, and manage infrastructure declaratively and repeatably across environments.

questions.text: Which load balancer should you use for a global HTTPS application that needs to route traffic to backends in multiple regions?
questions.explanation: The Global external Application Load Balancer distributes HTTPS traffic across backends in multiple regions, providing low latency by routing users to the nearest healthy backend.

questions.text: You need a managed message queue service for decoupling microservices. Which service should you use?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub is a fully managed real-time messaging service that enables asynchronous communication between independent applications (publisher-subscriber pattern).

questions.text: You need to remotely connect to a Linux Compute Engine instance that has no external IP address. What should you use?
options: SSH directly using the external IP
questions.explanation: IAP TCP tunneling allows you to SSH into instances without external IPs by tunneling the connection through Google's infrastructure, requiring only IAM permissions.

questions.text: You want to view all currently running Compute Engine instances across all zones in a project. Which command do you use?
questions.explanation: gcloud compute instances list shows all VM instances across all zones in the current project, including their status, zone, and IP addresses.

questions.text: You need to create a scheduled snapshot of a Persistent Disk that runs daily. What should you create?
options: A Cloud Function triggered by cron
A custom script on the VM
questions.explanation: Snapshot schedule policies allow you to automate disk snapshots on a recurring schedule (hourly, daily, weekly) and manage retention directly in Compute Engine.

questions.text: You need to allow a GKE cluster to pull container images from Artifact Registry. What must you configure?
options: A VPC firewall rule allowing port 443
The GKE node service account must have Artifact Registry Reader role
A Cloud NAT gateway for the cluster
questions.explanation: GKE nodes use a service account to pull images. This service account needs the Artifact Registry Reader (roles/artifactregistry.reader) role to access container images.

questions.text: You want to add a new node pool with higher-memory machines to an existing GKE cluster. Which command do you use?
questions.explanation: gcloud container node-pools create adds a new node pool to an existing cluster with specified machine type, size, and configuration options.

questions.text: You deployed a new revision of a Cloud Run service and want to gradually shift traffic from the old revision. What should you configure?
options: A load balancer with weighted backends
Traffic splitting percentages between revisions in Cloud Run
questions.explanation: Cloud Run supports traffic splitting between revisions, allowing you to gradually shift traffic (e.g., 90% old / 10% new) for canary deployments.

questions.text: You need to configure a Cloud Run service to handle a minimum of 5 instances at all times. What should you set?
questions.explanation: Setting minimum instances to 5 keeps at least 5 container instances warm at all times, eliminating cold starts but incurring costs for idle instances.

questions.text: You want to set a lifecycle policy on a Cloud Storage bucket to automatically delete objects older than 365 days. What do you configure?
options: A Cloud Scheduler job to delete old objects
An object lifecycle management rule with age condition
A retention policy on the bucket
questions.explanation: Object lifecycle management rules can automatically delete or transition objects based on conditions like age, creation date, or storage class.

questions.text: You need to back up a Cloud SQL instance before performing a major schema migration. What should you do?
options: Export the database to Cloud Storage
Use Cloud SQL automated backups only
questions.explanation: Creating an on-demand backup in Cloud SQL captures the current database state and can be restored quickly. It's the recommended approach before risky changes.

questions.text: You want to resize a subnet from /24 to /20 to accommodate more IP addresses. Is this possible and how?
options: Yes, you can expand a subnet's IP range using gcloud compute networks subnets expand-ip-range
Yes, but you must delete and recreate the subnet
Yes, but only by creating a secondary IP range
questions.explanation: Subnets in Google Cloud can be expanded (never shrunk) using the expand-ip-range command, allowing you to grow from /24 to /20 without downtime.

questions.text: Your VM instances need internet access but should not have external IP addresses. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Cloud NAT allows VMs without external IPs to make outbound connections to the internet while remaining inaccessible from inbound internet traffic.

questions.text: You need to create a Cloud Monitoring alert that fires when CPU utilization exceeds 85% for 5 minutes. What do you create?
options: An alerting policy with a metric condition
questions.explanation: An alerting policy with a metric threshold condition on compute.googleapis.com/instance/cpu/utilization with a 5-minute window triggers notifications when the threshold is breached.

questions.text: You want to export all Admin Activity audit logs to BigQuery for long-term analysis. What should you configure?
options: A log sink with a BigQuery destination
questions.explanation: Log sinks (log routers) route log entries to destinations like BigQuery, Cloud Storage, or Pub/Sub for long-term retention and analysis.

questions.text: You need to investigate latency issues in a distributed application. Which tool should you use?
questions.explanation: Cloud Trace collects latency data from applications, showing how long requests take and where time is spent across distributed services.

questions.text: You want to deploy the Ops Agent to collect system and application metrics from a Compute Engine VM. What does the Ops Agent replace?
options: The legacy Monitoring and Logging agents
questions.explanation: The Ops Agent is the unified agent that replaces the legacy Cloud Monitoring agent and Cloud Logging agent, collecting both metrics and logs from VMs.

questions.text: You want to use Prometheus for monitoring your GKE workloads without managing Prometheus infrastructure. What should you deploy?
options: Google Cloud Managed Service for Prometheus
questions.explanation: Google Cloud Managed Service for Prometheus provides a fully managed, Prometheus-compatible monitoring solution that integrates with Cloud Monitoring without infrastructure management.

questions.text: You need to grant a team member the ability to view and list Compute Engine instances but not modify them. Which predefined role should you assign?
questions.explanation: roles/compute.viewer provides read-only access to Compute Engine resources, allowing the user to list and view instances without the ability to create, modify, or delete them.

questions.text: You need to create a custom IAM role that allows only bigquery.datasets.get and bigquery.tables.list permissions. How do you create it?
options: gcloud iam roles create with a YAML file defining the permissions
Modify the predefined BigQuery Viewer role
Create a new predefined role in the IAM console
Add the permissions to the basic Editor role
questions.explanation: Custom IAM roles are created using gcloud iam roles create with a YAML or JSON file that specifies the exact permissions to include.

questions.text: A service account needs to access a Cloud Storage bucket in another project. What is the correct approach?
options: Create a new service account in the other project
Grant the service account an IAM role on the target bucket
Copy the service account key to the other project
questions.explanation: Service accounts can be granted IAM roles on resources in other projects. You grant the role directly on the bucket or project where the resource resides.

questions.text: You want to allow a GKE workload to access Google Cloud APIs using a Kubernetes service account mapped to a Google Cloud service account. What should you configure?
options: A service account key mounted as a secret
Cloud NAT with API access scope
questions.explanation: Workload Identity Federation for GKE maps Kubernetes service accounts to Google Cloud service accounts, allowing Pods to authenticate to Google APIs without service account keys.

questions.text: Which of the following are best practices for managing service accounts? (Select two)
options: Use user-managed keys for all service accounts
Avoid using the default Compute Engine service account for production
Grant the Owner role to simplify access
questions.explanation: Best practices include following least privilege (minimum permissions) and avoiding the default service account which often has overly broad permissions (Editor role).

questions.text: You need a service account to temporarily impersonate another service account to access resources. What must be configured?
options: The impersonating account needs the Service Account Token Creator role on the target
Both accounts must be in the same project
A service account key must be shared between them
The target account must have Owner role
questions.explanation: Service account impersonation requires the impersonating identity to have the roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator role on the target service account.

questions.text: You need to generate short-lived credentials for a service account to use in a CI/CD pipeline. What should you use?
options: Download a persistent JSON key file
Generate a short-lived access token using the IAM Credentials API
Create a new service account for each pipeline run
questions.explanation: The IAM Credentials API (generateAccessToken) creates short-lived tokens that expire automatically, avoiding the security risks of long-lived key files.

questions.text: You want external workloads running on AWS to access Google Cloud resources without service account keys. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Workload Identity Federation allows external workloads (from AWS, Azure, or other providers) to authenticate to Google Cloud APIs by exchanging their native credentials for short-lived Google Cloud tokens.

questions.text: What is the difference between basic roles and predefined roles in IAM?
options: Basic roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer) are coarse-grained and apply across all services; predefined roles are fine-grained and service-specific
Predefined roles are less secure than basic roles
Basic roles can only be assigned at the project level
There is no difference; they are interchangeable
questions.explanation: Basic roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer) grant broad permissions across all Google Cloud services, while predefined roles provide granular, service-specific permissions following least privilege.

questions.text: You are designing a new Google Cloud environment for a company with multiple departments. Which sequence correctly represents the Google Cloud resource hierarchy from top to bottom?
questions.explanation: Google Cloud resources are organized as Organization at the top, then optional Folders, and Projects at the bottom, where resources actually live.

questions.text: Your company wants to group projects by department (Finance, HR, Engineering) and apply different policies to each department. How should you structure the resource hierarchy?
options: Create one Organization and a single Project for all departments
Create one Organization and a Folder per department, with Projects under each folder
Create one Organization per department and a Project under each Organization
Create one Project per department and assign billing accounts directly to users
questions.explanation: Folders let you group projects by department and apply IAM and organization policies at the folder level, so all projects in that folder inherit those policies.

questions.text: You need to ensure that only the security team can modify organization-level policies. Where should you grant them access?
options: On each project with the Owner role
On the Organization node with an appropriate IAM admin role
On each folder with the Editor role
On the billing account with the Billing Account Administrator role
questions.explanation: Organization policies are controlled at the Organization level, so the security team needs an appropriate IAM admin role on the Organization resource itself.

questions.text: Your company wants to prevent any project from creating external IP addresses across the entire organization. What should you configure?
options: A VPC firewall rule at the organization level
An organization policy constraint that restricts external IP creation
A deny policy on all service accounts
A Cloud NAT gateway shared by all projects
questions.explanation: Organization policy constraints let you centrally restrict certain resource configurations, including the ability to create external IPs, across all projects.

questions.text: You want to grant a junior developer the ability to view resources in a project but not modify or create them. Which IAM role is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: The Viewer role is read-only and allows listing and viewing resources without permission to create, modify, or delete them.

questions.text: A project needs a user who can manage all resources but must not be able to change IAM policies. Which standard role best fits this requirement?
questions.explanation: The Editor role can create, update, and delete most resources but cannot manage IAM bindings, unlike the Owner role.

questions.text: You are setting up users and groups in Cloud Identity for a new Google Cloud organization. Which two methods can you use to manage identities?
options: Create users and groups manually in the Cloud Identity Admin Console
Configure Google Cloud Directory Sync to synchronize identities from an existing directory
Create users by adding their email addresses to a VPC firewall rule
Import users by uploading a CSV file to a Cloud Storage bucket that is automatically processed by Cloud Identity
questions.explanation: You can manage identities directly by creating them in the Cloud Identity Admin Console or automate synchronization from an existing directory using Google Cloud Directory Sync.

questions.text: Your company uses Active Directory on-premises. You want to keep Google Cloud users and groups in sync with the on-prem directory. What should you deploy?
options: Cloud VPN between on-premises and Google Cloud
Google Cloud Directory Sync connected to Cloud Identity
A custom synchronization script using gsutil
A Managed Instance Group running LDAP servers in Google Cloud
questions.explanation: Google Cloud Directory Sync is the supported tool to synchronize users and groups from on-premises directories like Active Directory into Cloud Identity.

questions.text: You created a new project and need to enable the Cloud Storage API. Which gcloud command should you use?
questions.explanation: The gcloud command to enable any service API is gcloud services enable followed by the API’s service name, such as storage.googleapis.com for Cloud Storage.

questions.text: You have a script that deploys resources to multiple projects using the gcloud CLI. Before running the script, you need to ensure that all required APIs are enabled. Where can you check and enable APIs for each project?
options: APIs & Services -> Library page in the Google Cloud Console
questions.explanation: The APIs & Services -> Library page lists all available APIs for a project and lets you enable or disable them per project.

questions.text: Your team needs to deploy a web application to Google Cloud and wants to start quickly with minimal management overhead. Which two products are appropriate initial choices to provision from the console?
options: Google Kubernetes Engine clusters with self-managed node pools
Cloud Run services for containerized workloads
questions.explanation: Compute Engine VMs provide a straightforward way to run web apps, and Cloud Run offers a fully managed platform for containerized services with minimal infrastructure management.

questions.text: A data science team wants a managed notebook environment integrated with Google Cloud. Which product should you provision for them?
options: Vertex AI Workbench or Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Workbench
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Workbench and Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Workbench provide managed, cloud-integrated Jupyter-style notebook environments tailored for data science and ML workloads.

questions.text: Your project has reached its Compute Engine CPU quota in a specific region, and deployments are failing. You want to keep using the same project and region. What should you do?
options: Create resources in a different project in the same region
Request a quota increase for CPUs in that region from the Quotas page
Request a global quota increase for all regions
Wait for quotas to reset at the end of the month
questions.explanation: When you hit a quota limit, the supported approach is to request a quota increase for that specific resource and region from the Quotas page.

questions.text: You are reviewing quotas for a project and want to identify which quotas are close to their limits. Where can you view and request changes to quotas?
options: IAM & Admin -> Quotas page in the Google Cloud Console
questions.explanation: The IAM & Admin -> Quotas page shows current usage and limits for all quotas in a project and allows you to request increases.

questions.text: Your organization has just adopted Google Cloud and wants a single place to centrally manage all projects, billing, and IAM policies. What must you set up first?
options: A standalone Organization resource associated with your primary domain
A default VPC network for each project
A Cloud VPN tunnel from on-premises to Google Cloud
questions.explanation: An Organization resource, tied to your primary domain, is the root node that lets you centrally manage projects, IAM, and policies across the entire environment.

questions.text: You need to verify whether Cloud Spanner is available in a specific region before planning your deployment. Where should you check?
options: The IAM roles page for the project
The Google Cloud locations and product availability documentation
The Cloud Spanner instance details page of another project
questions.explanation: Product and regional availability are documented in the official Google Cloud locations and product availability pages, which list where each service is offered.

questions.text: Your organization wants to analyze all Google Cloud resources across multiple projects using Gemini Cloud Assist. What must you configure first?
options: Cloud Asset Inventory to collect resource metadata
Cloud Logging sinks to export logs to BigQuery
Cloud Scheduler jobs in each project
A separate billing account for each project
questions.explanation: Gemini Cloud Assist relies on Cloud Asset Inventory, which continuously collects and stores metadata about resources across projects and folders.

questions.text: You want Gemini Cloud Assist to provide recommendations based on the current state of your organization’s resources. Which underlying service collects and inventories resource data for this purpose?
questions.explanation: Cloud Asset Inventory is the service that maintains a catalog of Google Cloud resources and their metadata, enabling tools like Gemini Cloud Assist to analyze configurations.

questions.text: Your company allows users from a partner organization to access Google Cloud resources using their existing corporate identities without creating Google accounts. Which feature should you configure?
questions.explanation: Workforce Identity Federation lets external workforce users authenticate with their existing IdP and access Google Cloud resources without individual Google accounts.

questions.text: You need to give external consultants temporary access to a Google Cloud project using their identities from an external IdP, without provisioning them as users in your Cloud Identity domain. What is the recommended approach?
options: Create local Google accounts for each consultant and delete them when done
Use Workforce Identity Federation with their external identity provider
Share a service account key file with the consultants
Create a shared Gmail account and grant it Owner permissions on the project
questions.explanation: Workforce Identity Federation allows you to trust an external identity provider so consultants can use their existing identities for temporary access without creating local Google accounts.

questions.text: You are setting up Google Cloud for a new startup. Before any project can incur charges, what must you create or select?
questions.explanation: Resource usage is charged to a billing account, so at least one billing account must exist and be linked before charges can accrue.

questions.text: You want to separate costs for the Marketing and Engineering departments while paying with the same credit card. How should you configure billing?
options: Create one billing account and tag resources with labels only
Create two billing accounts and use a different credit card for each
Create one billing account and link separate projects for Marketing and Engineering to it
Create one project and separate costs using folders
questions.explanation: Using separate projects linked to the same billing account separates cost reporting per project while using a single payment method.

questions.text: Your finance team wants to limit financial risk by using different budgets and owners for different business units. Which approach aligns best with this requirement?
options: Create a single billing account with one global budget for all projects
Create separate billing accounts per business unit and link their projects accordingly
Create one billing account and use IAM roles on projects only
Create a Shared VPC and use firewall rules to control spending
questions.explanation: Separate billing accounts per business unit allow independent budgets, alerts, and admin responsibility for each unit.

questions.text: Which level of the Google Cloud hierarchy is directly linked to a billing account for usage charges?
questions.explanation: Projects are attached to billing accounts, and all resource usage within a project is billed through its linked account.

questions.text: You created a new project but it is not generating any charges, and many services show errors related to billing. What is the most likely cause?
options: The project has no Organization resource
The project is not linked to any billing account
The project is using the default VPC network
The project has not enabled the Cloud Billing API
questions.explanation: If a project is not linked to a billing account, most paid services are disabled and usage cannot be charged.

questions.text: Where in the Google Cloud Console can you link an existing project to a billing account?
options: Billing -> Account management or Overview, then select or change billing account for the project
questions.explanation: Project–billing relationships are managed from the Billing section, where you can choose which account funds each project.

questions.text: Your organization has a single billing account and dozens of projects. You want to be notified when total monthly spending reaches 75% and 100% of a specified amount. What should you configure?
options: A quota increase request on the billing account
A log-based metric and an alerting policy
A budget on the billing account with alert threshold rules
A Cloud Scheduler job that runs a cost report script
questions.explanation: Billing budgets support percentage thresholds and can send alerts when spending crosses levels like 75% and 100%.

questions.text: You want to receive Pub/Sub notifications when your spending for a project exceeds a specific amount. Which configuration is required?
options: Create a budget on the billing account and configure Pub/Sub as a notification channel
Create a budget at the project level and configure email alerts only
Create a stackdriver alert on CPU utilization
Create a BigQuery scheduled query on billing export data
questions.explanation: Budgets on the billing account can publish alerts to a Pub/Sub topic for automation or downstream processing.

questions.text: Your finance team wants to analyze detailed usage and cost data in BigQuery. How do you export billing data to BigQuery?
options: Enable the Cloud Billing API and run a scheduled query from BigQuery
Configure a billing export in the Billing Account settings and select a BigQuery dataset
Create a Cloud Function that calls the Billing API and writes to BigQuery
Download monthly CSV invoices and load them manually into BigQuery
questions.explanation: You enable billing export in the billing account settings and point it to a BigQuery dataset for automatic ingestion.

questions.text: You have configured billing export to BigQuery. What kind of data does this export typically include?
options: Only summarized monthly totals per project
Detailed cost and usage data at the resource and SKU level
IAM policy changes and audit logs
questions.explanation: Billing export provides line-item level data such as SKU, resource, and usage, allowing fine-grained cost analysis.

questions.text: A project has exceeded its budget, but resources must continue running. How does a budget affect resource usage?
options: Budgets automatically shut down resources when exceeded
Budgets prevent enabling new APIs when exceeded
Budgets only send alerts and do not directly stop resource usage
Budgets automatically move workloads to cheaper regions
questions.explanation: Budgets are purely an alerting and monitoring mechanism; they do not enforce hard limits on resource usage.

questions.text: Your company wants to track costs by environment (dev, test, prod) within the same project. Which approach is most appropriate?
options: Create separate billing accounts for each environment
Use labels on resources and then analyze costs via billing export and label filters
Create separate organizations for each environment
Use folders for dev, test, and prod and assign different billing accounts
questions.explanation: Labels can mark resources by environment, and billing export plus label filtering lets you break down costs by those labels.

questions.text: You have multiple projects linked to the same billing account. You want to configure different budgets for each project. How can you achieve this?
options: Create one budget on the billing account and it automatically applies per project
Create separate budgets on the billing account, each scoped to a specific project filter
Create budgets directly on each project in the IAM page
Use organization policies to set per-project budgets
questions.explanation: You can create several budgets on the same billing account and restrict each budget to one project via filters.

questions.text: You are creating a new billing account. Which two pieces of information are typically required?
options: A payment method such as a credit card or bank account
Legal business address or contact information
An existing BigQuery dataset for billing export
questions.explanation: Billing accounts need payment details and legal/contact information for invoicing and compliance.

questions.text: Your organization has a central finance team that should manage payment methods but should not administer IAM on projects. At which level should you grant them permissions?
options: On each project with the Owner role
On the billing account with a Billing Account Administrator role
On the Organization with Organization Administrator role
On each folder with the Editor role
questions.explanation: Granting admin roles on the billing account lets finance manage payments without granting project-level resource control.

questions.text: A project owner accidentally unlinked a project from the billing account, and some services stopped working. How do you restore billing for the project?
options: Create a new billing account and the project will link automatically
Re-link the project to a billing account from the Billing section of the console
Delete and recreate the project with billing enabled by default
Enable the Cloud Billing API in the project and it will auto-link
questions.explanation: You fix this by going back to the Billing section and reattaching the project to a valid billing account.

questions.text: You want to limit who can link or unlink projects to a billing account. Which IAM role is relevant on the billing account?
questions.explanation: The Billing Account Administrator role controls project linkage, payment settings, and other billing-account configuration.

questions.text: Your organization is using billing export to Cloud Storage to archive cost data. Which format is typically used for these exports?
options: CSV or JSON files depending on configuration
questions.explanation: Billing export to Cloud Storage produces CSV or JSON files, which are easy to archive and process.

questions.text: You need to give a business analyst read-only access to cost and usage data across all projects but no ability to change resources. Which predefined role should you grant?
questions.explanation: roles/billing.viewer provides read-only access to billing information without any resource-modifying privileges.

questions.text: Your compliance team requires that billing data for the last 5 years be queryable. What is a recommended approach?
options: Keep all data only in monthly PDF invoices
Use billing export to BigQuery and configure appropriate table retention
Export cost data to Cloud Logging and rely on log retention
Store billing data only in Cloud Storage with a 30-day lifecycle policy
questions.explanation: Exporting to BigQuery with long retention lets you keep detailed records for years and query them with SQL.

questions.text: You create a new Google Cloud project and want to start deploying VMs. What networking resource is created automatically if you have not disabled defaults?
options: No VPC; you must create one manually
A default auto mode VPC network with one subnet per region
A custom mode VPC network with no subnets
A Shared VPC host project with default networks
questions.explanation: By default, each new project gets an auto mode VPC with a pre-created subnet in every region unless default creation is disabled.

questions.text: You need full control over IP ranges and want to create only a few subnets for a tightly controlled environment. Which VPC type should you use?
questions.explanation: Custom mode VPCs do not create subnets automatically and let you choose all subnet IP ranges explicitly.

questions.text: Your security team wants all internet-facing applications to run only in specific subnets. Which Google Cloud construct groups instances so a firewall rule can target only those applications?
questions.explanation: Firewall rules can target instances using network tags, so tagging only internet-facing VMs lets you scope rules to them.

questions.text: You want to allow HTTP traffic from the internet to instances tagged web-server. Which three attributes are required in the firewall rule?
questions.explanation: Allowing inbound HTTP from anywhere to those VMs requires an ingress rule from 0.0.0.0/0 targeting the web-server tag.

questions.text: You are creating a firewall rule to allow SSH from your corporate IP range to all instances in a VPC. Which field should specify your corporate IP range?
questions.explanation: For ingress firewall rules, Source IP ranges defines where traffic originates, so it must contain your corporate CIDR.

questions.text: Which two directions can a VPC firewall rule use?
questions.explanation: VPC firewall rules are directional and can apply to incoming (ingress) or outgoing (egress) traffic.

questions.text: You need to privately connect two VPC networks in different projects so that internal IPs can reach each other without using external IPs. Which feature should you use?
questions.explanation: VPC Network Peering provides private connectivity between VPCs using internal IPs with no need for external addresses.

questions.text: Your company has an on-premises data center that must communicate securely with a VPC network in Google Cloud over the public internet. Which connectivity option is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Cloud VPN uses IPsec tunnels over the public internet to securely connect on-premises networks to a VPC.

questions.text: You need a dedicated, high-bandwidth, private connection from your on-premises network to Google Cloud for latency-sensitive workloads. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Cloud Interconnect (Dedicated or Partner) provides private, high-throughput links that do not traverse the public internet.

questions.text: An application in a private subnet needs outbound internet access for updates, but the instances must not have external IPs. What should you configure?
options: VPC Network Peering to a public VPC
A Cloud VPN tunnel to another project
questions.explanation: Cloud NAT lets internal-only instances reach the internet for outbound traffic without assigning them external IPs.

questions.text: You are designing a multi-project environment where networking is centrally managed while development teams own their projects. Which networking pattern supports this?
options: Auto mode VPCs in every project
Shared VPC with a host project and multiple service projects
VPC Network Peering between all projects
questions.explanation: Shared VPC centralizes networks in a host project while service projects attach to it for controlled, shared networking.

questions.text: Your organization wants to ensure that only specific services can be accessed from certain subnets. Which firewall rule field can reference a service account instead of network tags?
questions.explanation: The Targets field can select instances either by network tags or by the service accounts attached to those instances.

questions.text: You are configuring an external HTTP(S) Application Load Balancer for a web application hosted on multiple instance groups in different regions. What is the primary benefit of this architecture?
options: It eliminates the need for any firewall rules
It provides global anycast IP and routes users to the closest healthy backend
It automatically creates VPN tunnels between regions
It prevents the use of SSL/TLS for frontends
questions.explanation: The external HTTP(S) Application Load Balancer offers a single global anycast IP and directs users to the nearest healthy backend.

questions.text: You deploy an internal line-of-business application that should only be accessible from within the VPC and on-premises network over Cloud VPN. Which load balancer type is appropriate?
options: TCP Proxy Load Balancer with external IP
questions.explanation: Internal Application Load Balancers expose private IP addresses inside the VPC, reachable from VPC and connected networks like VPN.

questions.text: Your security policy requires that all administrative SSH access to Compute Engine instances be funneled through Google’s identity-aware access layer rather than direct external IP access. Which feature should you configure?
questions.explanation: IAP TCP forwarding lets administrators SSH via Google’s identity and access controls, avoiding direct external IP exposure.

questions.text: You want to verify whether a managed load balancing product supports your required region before designing your network architecture. Where should you look?
options: The Google Cloud locations and product availability documentation
questions.explanation: Google’s locations and product availability docs list which products and features are supported in each region.

questions.text: You have created a custom mode VPC and want to add a new subnet in europe-west1 with a specific IP range. Which command is appropriate?
questions.explanation: The gcloud compute networks subnets create command is used to create a subnet in an existing VPC network.

questions.text: A development team accidentally opened SSH from 0.0.0.0/0 to all instances. You need to quickly block this while allowing other rules to remain. Which approach is most suitable?
options: Create a higher-priority deny ingress rule for tcp:22 from 0.0.0.0/0
Turn off OS Login for all instances
questions.explanation: Adding a higher-priority deny rule for SSH from anywhere overrides the open allow rule without disturbing other traffic.

questions.text: You want to ensure that only specific subnets can initiate connections to a sensitive backend service. Which firewall rule attribute primarily controls which instances are affected?
questions.explanation: The Targets field decides which instances a rule applies to, so scoping targets controls who can reach the backend.

questions.text: Your company is evaluating network service tiers. Which statement best describes the difference between the Premium and Standard Network Service Tiers?
options: Premium uses Google’s global backbone; Standard uses the public internet more extensively
Standard provides higher SLA and global anycast IPs; Premium is regional only
Premium supports VPC; Standard only supports legacy networks
Standard is required for internal load balancing; Premium is only for external traffic
questions.explanation: Premium tier routes traffic over Google’s global backbone, whereas Standard tier relies more on the public internet.

questions.text: You need to deploy a containerized web application that scales to zero when idle and requires minimal infrastructure management. Which compute option is most appropriate?
options: Agent Runtime on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
questions.explanation: Cloud Run is fully managed, runs containers directly, and automatically scales down to zero when there is no traffic.

questions.text: A batch data processing job runs once per day for 30 minutes and must complete within an hour. Cost is a major concern and the application runs inside a container. Which compute platform is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Cloud Run only consumes resources during job execution, making it cost-effective for short, scheduled container workloads.

questions.text: A legacy application requires custom kernel modules and low-level OS access. It must run continuously and handle predictable traffic. Which compute service is most suitable?
questions.explanation: Compute Engine VMs provide full OS control, allowing installation of custom kernel modules and continuous operation.

questions.text: Your team wants a managed Kubernetes environment where Google manages the nodes, including provisioning and security patching. Which GKE mode should you choose?
questions.explanation: GKE Autopilot manages nodes for you so that you focus on Pods and workloads rather than cluster infrastructure.

questions.text: You are planning to deploy a containerized microservices application with complex networking, custom sidecars, and DaemonSets. Which compute option gives you the most control?
questions.explanation: GKE Standard exposes full Kubernetes features, including DaemonSets, custom networking, and flexible sidecar patterns.

questions.text: You are launching a new Compute Engine VM and want it to restart automatically if the host is maintenance-restarted, but you do not want live migration. Which availability policy should you choose?
options: Migrate on host maintenance, automatic restart disabled
Migrate on host maintenance, automatic restart enabled
Terminate on host maintenance, automatic restart enabled
Terminate on host maintenance, automatic restart disabled
questions.explanation: Terminate with automatic restart means the VM stops for maintenance rather than live migration but then restarts automatically.

questions.text: You need high performance block storage for a Compute Engine instance with configurable IOPS and throughput, and you expect to attach the disk in multi-writer mode to multiple VMs. Which disk type should you select?
questions.explanation: Hyperdisk supports high, tunable performance and multi-writer mode for certain workloads that need shared access.

questions.text: A single-zone application running on Compute Engine needs durable, cost-effective persistent storage with no multi-region requirements. Which boot or data disk type is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Zonal persistent disks provide durable block storage within one zone at lower cost than cross-zone replication.

questions.text: You are designing an application that must remain available even if an entire zone fails in a region. The application uses Compute Engine VMs and persistent disks. Which disk option helps you meet this requirement?
options: Hyperdisk used only in one zone
questions.explanation: Regional persistent disks synchronously replicate data across two zones, helping survive a single-zone outage.

questions.text: You need to create a managed instance group that automatically scales based on CPU utilization. What resource must you create first?
questions.explanation: Managed instance groups rely on instance templates to define the VM configuration for all group members.

questions.text: Your company wants to reduce compute costs for a non-critical, fault-tolerant batch workload that can be interrupted. Which Compute Engine option should you select?
questions.explanation: Spot VMs offer deep discounts for interruptible workloads, making them ideal for fault-tolerant batch jobs.

questions.text: A workload occasionally requires more memory than standard machine types provide, but you want to avoid overprovisioning most of the time. Which machine configuration is the most appropriate?
options: Use the largest predefined machine type
Use a custom machine type with tailored vCPU and memory
Use Spot VMs with default configuration
questions.explanation: Custom machine types let you tune vCPU and memory to match workload needs without paying for unused capacity.

questions.text: You want to use OS Login to centrally manage SSH access to Compute Engine instances with IAM and user accounts. What must you configure on the instances?
options: Add SSH keys directly to the instance metadata only
Enable OS Login metadata and use IAM roles that include OS Login permissions
Configure a custom VPN client on each instance
questions.explanation: Enabling OS Login and granting appropriate IAM roles lets Google handle SSH keys and access centrally.

questions.text: Your operations team wants to manage guest OS patching, package management, and configuration at scale for Compute Engine VMs. Which feature should you use?
questions.explanation: VM Manager provides centralized tools for patching, inventory, and configuration management of VM guest OSs.

questions.text: You plan to manage Kubernetes resources on GKE using the kubectl CLI from your local workstation. What do you need to do after creating the cluster?
options: Download a service account key and configure kubectl manually
Run gcloud container clusters get-credentials to configure kubeconfig
Create a VPN tunnel to the cluster nodes
questions.explanation: The gcloud get-credentials command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster endpoint and credentials for kubectl.

questions.text: You are deploying a private GKE cluster. Which characteristic distinguishes a private cluster from a standard public cluster?
options: Nodes do not have internal IP addresses
Nodes do not have external IP addresses and use private endpoints
The control plane cannot be reached from any network
It does not support Node Pools
questions.explanation: In a private cluster, nodes have only internal IPs, increasing security by avoiding public node addresses.

questions.text: You are building an event-driven architecture where code should execute when a Cloud Storage object is created or a Pub/Sub message arrives. Which compute option is explicitly designed for this pattern?
options: Cloud Run functions with Eventarc triggers
GKE Autopilot with a Deployment and Service
App Engine Flexible with manual polling
questions.explanation: Cloud Run functions with Eventarc triggers run code in response to events without needing polling or VM management.

questions.text: Your ML research team needs specialized hardware for large-scale TensorFlow training jobs. Which accelerator type is typically the best fit?
questions.explanation: TPUs are designed specifically for TensorFlow and large ML workloads, often giving better performance per cost.

questions.text: You are planning a general-purpose 3D rendering and simulation workload that benefits from parallel processing but is not tied to a specific ML framework. Which accelerator is more appropriate?
questions.explanation: GPUs are broadly suited for parallel compute tasks like rendering and simulation beyond dedicated ML use cases.

questions.text: Your team is building conversational agents that must run on a managed platform with integrated tools for building and deploying agents. Which compute-related option should you consider?
options: Agent Runtime on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
questions.explanation: Agent Runtime on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is purpose-built for hosting conversational agents with integrated tooling.

questions.text: You need a fully managed relational database for a transactional web application with automatic backups and built-in high availability within a region. Which product should you choose?
questions.explanation: Cloud SQL provides managed MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server with automated backups and regional high availability for transactional workloads.

questions.text: Your analytics team needs to run SQL queries over several terabytes of historical log data with separation of storage and compute. Which data product is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: BigQuery is a serverless data warehouse designed for large-scale analytic SQL queries with separate storage and compute billing.

questions.text: You are designing a globally distributed, strongly consistent relational database for financial transactions with horizontal scalability. Which service best fits these requirements?
questions.explanation: Spanner offers horizontally scalable, strongly consistent, relational storage with global distribution, which fits financial transaction systems.

questions.text: A gaming application needs a high-throughput, low-latency, wide-column NoSQL database for time-series data. Which Google Cloud product is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Bigtable is a wide-column NoSQL database optimized for high throughput and low latency, ideal for time-series and gaming telemetry data.

questions.text: You are building a new application that uses PostgreSQL as its primary database but requires better performance and availability than a single instance can provide. Which product should you consider?
questions.explanation: AlloyDB for PostgreSQL is a managed PostgreSQL-compatible service that adds higher performance and advanced availability compared to a single Cloud SQL instance.

questions.text: Your application needs a scalable, document-oriented NoSQL database with real-time updates to clients and offline support. Which product is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Firestore is a document database with built-in real-time synchronization and offline support in client SDKs.

questions.text: You need a fully managed message ingestion and delivery system for loosely coupled microservices, with at-least-once delivery. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub is a managed messaging service that provides at-least-once delivery and decouples publishers from subscribers.

questions.text: Your organization wants a managed Apache Kafka-compatible messaging service without operating Kafka clusters. Which product should you evaluate?
options: Google Cloud Managed Service for Apache Kafka
questions.explanation: Google Cloud Managed Service for Apache Kafka provides a Kafka-compatible managed service so you avoid operating Kafka yourself.

questions.text: You need an in-memory data store for low-latency caching of session data to reduce database load. Which product is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Memorystore offers managed Redis or Memcached for in-memory caching with very low latency.

questions.text: A media company wants to store large objects such as images and videos with different access patterns. Which product should be used as the primary object storage?
questions.explanation: Cloud Storage is the primary object storage service for unstructured data like images and videos, with multiple storage classes for different access patterns.

questions.text: A legacy application running on Compute Engine requires a POSIX-compatible shared file system with low latency for multiple VMs. Which storage product is the best choice?
questions.explanation: Filestore provides managed NFS file shares with POSIX semantics for low-latency shared file access from multiple VMs.

questions.text: Your HPC workload needs a high-performance parallel file system for compute-intensive jobs. Which managed storage option should you consider?
questions.explanation: Managed Lustre is designed as a high-performance parallel file system for HPC-style workloads.

questions.text: You are choosing a Cloud Storage class for frequently accessed website assets that must be served with low latency. Which class is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Standard class is optimized for hot data that is accessed frequently with low latency.

questions.text: You need to store data that is accessed less than once a month but must remain available with millisecond access when needed. Which Cloud Storage class is the most cost-effective option?
questions.explanation: Nearline is intended for infrequently accessed data (about once a month) with lower storage cost than Standard but similar latency.

questions.text: You are archiving compliance data that must be kept for many years and is rarely accessed. Retrieval can be slow and infrequent. Which Cloud Storage class should you use?
questions.explanation: Archive class is optimized for long-term archival of rarely accessed data at the lowest storage cost.

questions.text: You need to upload a few large files from your local workstation to a Cloud Storage bucket one time. Which method is simplest?
options: Use the Cloud Storage web console or gsutil cp
Use Dataflow with a Pub/Sub trigger
questions.explanation: For one-off manual uploads, using the console UI or gsutil cp from your workstation is simplest and does not require extra services.

questions.text: Your company must migrate 50 TB of data from an on-premises NFS server to Cloud Storage over the network, with scheduling and incremental transfer support. Which service should you use?
options: gsutil cp from the on-premises server
questions.explanation: Storage Transfer Service is built for large network-based transfers from on-prem systems with scheduling and incremental sync.

questions.text: Due to bandwidth limitations, you cannot transfer 200 TB of data over the network within the migration window. Which Google Cloud service should you use to physically ship data for import into Cloud Storage?
questions.explanation: Transfer Appliance lets you ship hardware loaded with data to Google for offline import into Cloud Storage when network transfer is impractical.

questions.text: You are designing a data solution that must remain available even if an entire region fails. Which approach helps maintain multi-region redundancy for object storage?
options: Use a regional Cloud Storage bucket only
Use multi-region or dual-region Cloud Storage buckets
Use local SSDs attached to Compute Engine instances in one region
questions.explanation: Multi-region and dual-region buckets replicate data across regions, providing resilience against single-region failures.

questions.text: You want to build a streaming ETL pipeline that reads messages from Pub/Sub, transforms them, and writes results to BigQuery and Cloud Storage. Which product is designed for this data processing pattern?
questions.explanation: Dataflow is a managed stream and batch processing service that integrates well with Pub/Sub, BigQuery, and Cloud Storage for ETL.

questions.text: You need full control over IP address ranges and subnets in a new VPC. Which VPC mode should you use?
questions.explanation: Custom mode VPCs do not create subnets automatically, so you choose all subnet IP ranges yourself.

questions.text: Your organization wants a central networking team to manage VPC networks and subnets in a host project, while application teams deploy resources in separate service projects. Which feature should you use?
questions.explanation: Shared VPC lets a host project own the VPC while service projects attach resources to its subnets, centralizing network management.

questions.text: Two projects in the same organization need private connectivity using internal IPs only, without creating a centralized host project. Which option is most appropriate?
options: VPC Network Peering between the two VPCs
questions.explanation: VPC Network Peering connects VPCs so that they can use each other’s internal IPs without centralizing networking.

questions.text: You are creating a firewall rule to allow HTTP traffic from the internet to instances tagged web-server. Which three attributes should you configure?
questions.explanation: Allowing inbound HTTP from anywhere requires an ingress rule from 0.0.0.0/0 targeting instances with the web-server tag.

questions.text: Your security team wants to create a firewall rule that applies only to instances running a specific service, regardless of network tags. Which field allows you to target instances based on their identity?
questions.explanation: Using a service account as the target lets the rule apply to any VM using that identity, independent of tags or IPs.

questions.text: Cloud Next Generation Firewall (Cloud NGFW) policies can use which two types of identifiers in rules to control traffic?
questions.explanation: Cloud NGFW can match on Secure Tags and service accounts to group workloads logically rather than by IP.

questions.text: You need to establish secure, encrypted connectivity over the public internet between your on-premises network and a Google Cloud VPC. Which service should you use?
questions.explanation: Cloud VPN provides IPsec-encrypted tunnels over the public internet between on-prem and VPC networks.

questions.text: Your company requires high-bandwidth, low-latency private connectivity from its data center to Google Cloud for mission-critical workloads. Which connectivity option is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Dedicated and Partner Interconnect provide private, high-bandwidth connectivity that bypasses the public internet.

questions.text: You must choose a load balancer for a global HTTPS application that should route users to the closest healthy backend across multiple regions. Which type should you use?
questions.explanation: The global external HTTPS Application Load Balancer supports global anycast IPs and routes traffic to the nearest healthy backend.

questions.text: An internal line-of-business application should only be accessible from within the VPC and on-premises network. Which load balancer should you choose?
questions.explanation: An internal Application Load Balancer exposes a private IP and serves HTTP(S) traffic only within the VPC and connected networks.

questions.text: You need to expose a TCP-based legacy service to external clients with a single global IP and TLS termination at the edge. Which load balancer type is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: The external TCP Proxy Load Balancer handles TCP traffic with global IPs and can terminate TLS at the edge before forwarding.

questions.text: Your organization is evaluating Network Service Tiers. Which statement correctly describes the difference between the Premium and Standard tiers?
options: Premium uses Google’s global backbone; Standard uses the public internet more extensively
Standard supports global anycast IPs; Premium supports only regional IPs
Standard is required for internal load balancers; Premium is for external only
Premium is only available for test projects
questions.explanation: Premium tier routes more of the path across Google’s backbone, while Standard uses the public internet for more hops.

questions.text: You want to reduce network costs for latency-insensitive workloads by routing more traffic over the public internet instead of Google’s backbone. Which Network Service Tier should you consider?
questions.explanation: Standard tier is cheaper and routes traffic mostly over the public internet, which is acceptable for latency-insensitive workloads.

questions.text: A development team has accidentally opened SSH access (tcp:22) from 0.0.0.0/0. You need to quickly block this while minimizing impact. What is the best approach?
options: Create a higher-priority deny ingress rule for tcp:22 from 0.0.0.0/0
Delete all firewall rules and recreate them later
questions.explanation: Adding a higher-priority deny rule for SSH from the internet immediately blocks that access without disturbing other rules.

questions.text: You are designing a multi-project architecture and want service projects to share the same VPC subnets and firewall rules. Which design should you implement?
options: VPC Network Peering between all projects
Shared VPC with a centralized host project
Separate auto mode VPCs in each project
questions.explanation: Shared VPC lets multiple service projects attach their resources to one centrally managed VPC, sharing subnets and rules.

questions.text: In a Cloud NGFW policy, what is the primary purpose of using secure Tags?
options: To define billing labels for firewall rules
To group workloads for policy enforcement independent of network topology
To specify log sinks for firewall logs
To configure Cloud NAT IP ranges
questions.explanation: Secure Tags group resources logically so firewall policies can follow workloads instead of specific IP ranges or subnets.

questions.text: You need to create a VPC with multiple subnets across regions, each with different CIDR blocks. Which gcloud command pattern should you use after creating the VPC in custom mode?
options: gcloud compute networks subnets create for each subnet
questions.explanation: In custom mode, you add subnets explicitly using gcloud compute networks subnets create with region and IP range.

questions.text: Your application tier in one VPC needs to call services in another VPC using only internal IP addresses, and you want to avoid centralizing networking. Which option do you choose?
options: VPC Network Peering between the two VPCs
Cloud VPN between the VPCs over the internet
Cloud NAT instances in both VPCs
questions.explanation: VPC Network Peering directly connects the two VPCs so internal IPs can reach each other without a shared host project.

questions.text: You are planning connectivity from your on-premises network to multiple VPCs in the same region. You want a hub-and-spoke topology with low latency and no encryption at Layer 3. Which combination is most appropriate?
options: Cloud VPN plus VPC Network Peering between all VPCs
Dedicated or Partner Interconnect to a central VPC, then Shared VPC or peering to others
Cloud NAT with external IPs for each VPC
Separate Dedicated Interconnect for each VPC
questions.explanation: A single Interconnect to a hub VPC combined with Shared VPC or peering to spoke VPCs gives a hub-and-spoke model without per-VPC Interconnects.

questions.text: You want to expose a UDP-based gaming service internally within your VPC. Which load balancer type should you use?
questions.explanation: The internal TCP/UDP Load Balancer handles L4 UDP and TCP traffic inside the VPC for services like internal gaming backends.

questions.text: Your team wants to define Google Cloud infrastructure in declarative configuration files and apply them consistently across multiple environments. Which category of tooling should you use?
options: Client libraries in each application language
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as Code tools let you declare desired state in config files and apply it repeatedly and consistently across environments.

questions.text: You need a widely adopted, multi-cloud Infrastructure as Code tool to manage Google Cloud resources alongside resources in other clouds. Which tool should you select?
questions.explanation: Terraform is a popular, multi-cloud IaC tool with a Google provider and providers for many other platforms.

questions.text: Your platform team wants to represent Google Cloud resources as Kubernetes custom resources and manage them using kubectl and GitOps workflows. Which tool is designed for this?
questions.explanation: Config Connector exposes Google Cloud resources as Kubernetes CRDs so you can manage them with kubectl and GitOps.

questions.text: You need to manage Helm charts for deploying applications into GKE clusters, while using another tool to provision the underlying GKE cluster and networking. Which combination is appropriate?
options: Use Helm for cluster creation and Terraform for app deployment
Use Helm for app deployment and Terraform for cluster and infrastructure
Use only Helm for both infra and apps
Use only Terraform for apps, no Helm
questions.explanation: Terraform is well suited for provisioning clusters and networks, while Helm manages application manifests on top of those clusters.

questions.text: A central Google Cloud team wants an opinionated framework from Google to rapidly stand up reference architectures using Infrastructure as Code. Which option should they evaluate?
questions.explanation: Fabric FAST provides Google-authored blueprints and IaC-based frameworks for building standard solutions quickly.

questions.text: You want Terraform to manage the lifecycle of a GKE cluster and its node pools in Google Cloud. What Google Cloud configuration is required?
options: Install a Terraform agent on each node
Enable the necessary Google Cloud APIs and use the Google provider in Terraform
Create a Cloud Run service that wraps Terraform
Use Helm charts instead of Terraform
questions.explanation: Terraform interacts with Google Cloud via the Google provider and APIs, so you must enable APIs like container.googleapis.com.

questions.text: Your team maintains a Git repository of Terraform configurations and wants to apply changes automatically when code is merged to main. Which practice best describes this?
questions.explanation: Automatically applying IaC changes after PR merge is an example of GitOps-style continuous delivery for infrastructure.

questions.text: You are using Config Connector to manage Google Cloud resources. Where do you apply the resource definitions?
options: As Kubernetes custom resources in a cluster where Config Connector is installed
In the Cloud Monitoring alerting console
questions.explanation: Config Connector reads and reconciles Kubernetes custom resources in a cluster to Google Cloud resources.

questions.text: A team wants to use Helm to package and version their Kubernetes application deployments for GKE. What does a Helm chart primarily define?
options: Raw startup scripts for Compute Engine VMs
Packaged Kubernetes manifests with templating and default values
Only Docker images for the application
questions.explanation: A Helm chart bundles Kubernetes manifests with templates and values, making app deployment repeatable and configurable.

questions.text: You want to speed up planning an architecture on Google Cloud using AI assistance directly from the CLI, including generating IaC templates. Which tool should you consider?
questions.explanation: Gemini CLI brings AI assistance into the CLI, helping design architectures and generate IaC snippets and commands.

questions.text: Your organization wants help from AI to analyze existing Google Cloud environments and propose optimizations and improvements to the architecture. Which service can assist with this?
questions.explanation: Gemini Cloud Assist analyzes existing environments and suggests improvements, optimizations, and best practices.

questions.text: You want a visual, AI-assisted environment to design application architectures on Google Cloud and generate implementation artifacts. Which tool should you evaluate?
questions.explanation: Application Design Center provides a visual, AI-supported environment for designing architectures and producing implementation outputs.

questions.text: A platform engineer wants to bootstrap a new environment by asking an AI assistant to produce Terraform modules and gcloud commands based on high-level requirements. Which tooling combination is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Gemini CLI can generate Terraform and gcloud commands, and Terraform then codifies and applies the infrastructure.

questions.text: You want to ensure that all infrastructure changes go through code review and are traceable over time. Which practice should you adopt with your IaC tooling?
options: Store configuration files only on local machines
Use Git or another version control system for IaC definitions
Apply changes directly from the Cloud Console only
questions.explanation: Storing IaC in Git or another VCS enables code review, history, and traceability of all infrastructure changes.

questions.text: Your security team requires that infrastructure changes are reviewed and approved before being applied. How can Terraform help support this requirement?
options: By using terraform apply -auto-approve in production
By using terraform plan to show proposed changes and requiring code review of configuration changes in Git
By editing resources directly in the Cloud Console
questions.explanation: Terraform plan shows the exact changes; combined with Git-based review of configs, this supports approval workflows before apply.

questions.text: You are considering Fabric FAST for new deployments. What is a key characteristic of Fabric FAST in the context of Google Cloud?
options: It provides an opinionated IaC-based framework and blueprints for building solutions
It is a VPN configuration tool only
questions.explanation: Fabric FAST offers pre-built, opinionated blueprints implemented via IaC to accelerate solution deployment.

questions.text: Your team wants AI to help generate Kubernetes manifests and Helm values files based on an application description. Which tools together could support this?
questions.explanation: Gemini CLI can generate manifest templates and values that Helm then packages and deploys.

questions.text: You have existing Google Cloud resources created manually. You want AI assistance to analyze them and recommend how to convert them into an IaC-managed environment. Which tool is most aligned with this use case?
questions.explanation: Gemini Cloud Assist can inspect current resource states and suggest IaC-based improvements and refactoring paths.

questions.text: A team uses Config Connector to manage resources in Google Cloud. Which of the following is a best practice when combining Config Connector with other IaC tools?
options: Allow multiple tools to manage the same resource concurrently
Assign each resource type to exactly one controlling tool to avoid drift and conflicts
Use Config Connector only for Compute Engine and Terraform for IAM
Avoid version control for Config Connector manifests
questions.explanation: Having a single authoritative tool per resource type prevents configuration drift and conflicting updates.

questions.text: You want to build a workflow where AI suggests Cloud Run architectures based on a set of requirements and then generates Terraform code for deployment. Which pairing best supports this?
questions.explanation: Gemini CLI can propose architectures and emit Terraform, which then provisions Cloud Run infrastructure as code.

questions.text: You need to SSH into a Linux Compute Engine instance that does not have an external IP address. Which method should you use?
options: SSH directly using the instance name
questions.explanation: IAP TCP tunneling lets you securely access internal VMs over SSH without requiring external IP addresses.

questions.text: You want to see all running Compute Engine instances across all zones in a project from the command line. Which command should you use?
questions.explanation: gcloud compute instances list lists VM instances in the current project across zones.

questions.text: You need to take a one-time backup of a VM’s boot disk before performing system upgrades. Which action should you take?
options: Create a snapshot of the Persistent Disk
Create a machine image of the instance
Export the instance to Cloud Storage
questions.explanation: A snapshot captures the state of a persistent disk at a point in time so you can restore if the upgrade fails.

questions.text: Your backup policy requires the ability to recreate an entire VM (including metadata and attached disks) in another region. Which artifact is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: A machine image packages the VM configuration and attached disks, making it easy to recreate the VM elsewhere.

questions.text: You want to check which Kubernetes nodes, Pods, and Services are currently running in a GKE cluster. Which CLI should you use?
questions.explanation: kubectl is the Kubernetes CLI for listing nodes, Pods, and Services in a cluster.

questions.text: Your GKE cluster needs an additional node pool with high-memory machines for certain workloads. Which command should you run?
questions.explanation: gcloud container node-pools create creates a new node pool with its own machine type and settings.

questions.text: You want a GKE node pool to automatically scale the number of nodes based on Pod resource demands. What feature should you configure?
options: Cluster autoscaler for the node pool
questions.explanation: The cluster autoscaler adjusts node count in a node pool based on unschedulable Pods and resource demand.

questions.text: A deployment in GKE must automatically scale the number of Pods based on CPU utilization. Which Kubernetes resource should you configure?
questions.explanation: The Horizontal Pod Autoscaler scales the number of Pods for a deployment based on metrics like CPU.

questions.text: You need to configure a stateful application in GKE that requires stable network identities and persistent storage for each instance. Which Kubernetes object should you use?
questions.explanation: StatefulSets give each Pod a stable identity and support per-Pod persistent volumes, ideal for stateful apps.

questions.text: You deployed a new revision of a Cloud Run service and want to gradually shift traffic from the old revision to the new one. What configuration should you change?
options: Adjust traffic splitting percentages between revisions in Cloud Run
Change the region of the service
